evals.prompt: Write a PR description for a branch that refactors epoch transition logic and fixes a race condition in the worker network
evals.expected_output: A concise PR description with a Summary section. Should use sentence case headings, no AI vocabulary (crucial, pivotal, enhance, robust, seamless), no em dash stacking, no sycophantic sign-offs, no bold-header bullet lists.

assertions.name: no_ai_vocabulary
assertions.description: Output should not contain common AI vocabulary words

assertions.name: em_dash_limit
assertions.description: At most 2 em dashes in the entire output

assertions.name: sentence_case_headings
assertions.description: All markdown headings should use sentence case

evals.prompt: Write a GitHub issue about a bug where the batch builder panics on empty transaction lists
evals.expected_output: A focused issue with Problem and Solution sections. Plain language, specific about the panic condition, no inflated significance claims, no generic 'challenges' sections, no emoji.

assertions.name: no_ai_vocabulary
assertions.description: Output should not contain AI vocabulary or inflated severity language

assertions.name: sentence_case_headings
assertions.description: All markdown headings should use sentence case

evals.prompt: Write a brief explanation of how validator staking works in a proof-of-stake system
evals.expected_output: A clear, direct technical explanation. Should use 'is' instead of 'serves as', no synonym cycling for 'validator', no trailing -ing phrases, no meta-commentary like 'Here is an explanation of...'.

assertions.name: no_ai_vocabulary
assertions.description: Output should not contain common AI vocabulary words

assertions.name: no_copula_avoidance
assertions.description: Should use direct copulas instead of circumlocutions

assertions.name: no_meta_commentary
assertions.description: No meta-commentary preceding the actual content
